engagement-lifecycle.description: Three-phase lifecycle of a shadowrun engagement with key rules and guidelines

rows.name: The Meet
keyRules: Project confidence, not bravado
Dress for the meeting location, not for the Johnson
Holster all weapons visibly; max outerwear is an armored jacket
Never use magic during meetings
Ask all mission questions now; do not ask about the Johnson or employer
Determine three things: job details, payment intent, kill intent
Never hand over goods until paid
warningSignsOrGuidance: Money is too good or Johnson does not negotiate
Johnson shows up with overwhelming bodyguards
Johnson acts aggressively during the meeting
Johnson has a history of killing teams (check background)

rows.name: The Run
keyRules: Minimize contact with Mr. Johnson; default is 'don't call me'
Every communication risks exposure for both sides
Increase discretion as mission progresses
Only call Johnson if mission is fundamentally compromised
Solving problems independently is a mark of professionalism
Get paid almost entirely before completing irreversible actions (e.g., killing a target)
Aim for 80% payment before final action, not 20%
warningSignsOrGuidance: Targets may detect surveillance and begin counter-investigation
Contacting your Johnson may lead surveillance to the Johnson and back to you
Johnsons don't attack other Johnsons; shadowrunners are fair game
CFD complications are a valid reason to call

rows.name: The Handoff
keyRules: Never ask for more than was agreed
Control the environment: choose the meeting location
Set up tactical advantages (sniper overwatch, hacker scanning, visible weapons)
Use intimidation to ensure payment
Prefer runs where you hold a physical deliverable for leverage
Check Johnson's rep in the shadows before the handoff
warningSignsOrGuidance: Sabotage and assassination runs offer no physical leverage at handoff
Johnsons who don't pay get bad rep
Killing any team (even green runners) is taboo in the shadow community
Veteran teams can tolerate a Johnson not paying green runners, but killing is never acceptable

johnson-fixer-roles.description: Definitions and roles of Mr. Johnson and Fixers in the shadowrun ecosystem

rows.definition: Anyone who hires a shadowrunner for a job
keyAttributes: Becomes a criminal by hiring for illegal work
May not intend to pay or may intend to liquidate the team
rows.relationship: Employer of shadowrunners; client of fixers

rows.definition: Middleman providing goods and services to both Johnsons and shadowrunners
keyAttributes: Builds a stable of reliable shadowrunners
Provides ancillary services (street docs, arms dealers, etc.)
rows.relationship: Intermediary between Johnsons and shadowrunners; talent agency

fixer-payment-models.description: How fixers get compensated for connecting Johnsons with shadowrunners

rows.description: Johnson pays entire sum to fixer; fixer pays runners whatever he can get away with. Fixer effectively becomes Mr. Johnson.
rows.disadvantage: Payment tied to mission completion; increased risk; incentivizes micro-management

rows.description: Fixer charges Johnson a fee for assembling a crew, then steps out of the equation.
rows.advantage: Fixer gets paid quickly and moves to next deal

rows.description: Fixer charges a finder's fee to both the supplier and the runner for connecting them.
rows.advantage: Additional revenue stream on top of core business
rows.disadvantage: Fee is negotiable; both parties may push back

rows.description: Fixer trades for favors instead of liquid nuyen when cash-strapped.
rows.advantage: Builds social capital and long-term relationships

meet-etiquette.description: Rules of etiquette for the initial meeting with Mr. Johnson

rows.rule: Demonstrate expertise by asking expert questions
Show backbone; do not be a pushover
Dress for the meeting location, not for the Johnson
Fancy restaurant: dress well. Alley meeting: dress normally
Exception: high-society infiltration jobs benefit from showing high-society appearance
Holster all weapons: pistols in holsters, SMGs in slings, rifles slung
Maximum outerwear: armored jacket and concealable weapons
No need to actually conceal from Mr. J; just show effort to holster
Johnsons are well-versed in magic and wary of mood/mind-control spells
Using magic earns a serious black mark if discovered
Ask all mission questions during The Meet
Do not ask about the Johnson, employer, or motives
Focus on operational details: job specifics, guards, reinforcements, timing, security
